2605.04970.title: Skill Neologisms: Towards Skill-based Continual Learning
2605.04970.paper_summary: Modern LLMs show mastery over an ever-growing range of skills, as well as the ability to compose them flexibly. However, extending model capabilities to new skills in a scalable manner is an open-problem: fine-tuning and parameter-efficient variants risk catastrophic forgetting, while context-based approaches have limited expressiveness and are constrained by the model's effective context. We explore skill neologisms--i.e., soft tokens integrated in the model's vocabulary and optimized to improve capabilities over a specific skill--as a way to selectively extend model capabilities to new skills without weight updates. We first observe that off-the-shelf pre-trained LLMs already demonstrate tokens associated with procedural knowledge. We then show that skill neologisms can be learned to improve model capabilities on specific skills while being composable with out-of-distribution skills, and that independently trained skill neologisms can be composed zero-shot. These results suggest that skill neologisms may provide a scalable path towards skill-based continual learning.
2605.04970.paper_summary_zh: 現代的 LLM 展現了對不斷增長的技能範圍的掌握，以及靈活組合這些技能的能力。<lb/>然而，以可擴展的方式將模型能力擴展到新技能仍然是一個未解決的問題：微調和參數高效變體存在災難性遺忘的風險，而基於上下文的方法表達能力有限，並受到模型有效上下文的限制。<lb/>我們探索技能新詞——即集成在模型詞彙中的軟標記，並優化以提升特定技能的能力——作為選擇性擴展模型能力到新技能而不需要更新權重的一種方式。<lb/>我們首先觀察到現成的預訓練 LLM 已經展示了與程序性知識相關的標記。<lb/>然後，我們展示了技能新詞可以被學習以改善模型在特定技能上的能力，同時能與分佈外的技能進行組合，並且獨立訓練的技能新詞可以零樣本組合。<lb/>這些結果表明，技能新詞可能提供了一條可擴展的途徑，朝向基於技能的持續學習。
2605.04970.author: Antonin Berthon et.al.
2605.04970.authors: Antonin Berthon, Nicolas Astorga, Mihaela van der Schaar
